ddls:
CREATE TABLE "data_storage_systems" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data storage system */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the data storage system (e.g., RabbitMQ, Kafka, MySQL) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data storage system */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of data storage system (e.g., message broker, database, file system) */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Name of the vendor or company that provides the data storage system */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL to the official website or documentation */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the system information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system version */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data storage system this version belongs to */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the data storage system (e.g., 20.12.7.3) */,
  "patch_level" TEXT /* Patch level of the version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "release_notes" TEXT /* Release notes or changelog for the version */,
  "end_of_life" TEXT /* Date when the version will no longer be supported */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_versions_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES data_storage_systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "capabilities" (
  "capability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each capability */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system version this capability belongs to */,
  "capability_name" TEXT /* Name of the capability (e.g., supports_settings, supports_skipping_indices) */,
  "supported" INTEGER /* Whether the capability is supported by the system version */,
  "capability_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the capability */,
  "added_in_version" TEXT /* Version in which the capability was first supported */,
  "removed_in_version" TEXT /* Version in which the capability was removed (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("capability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_capabilities_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES system_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who interacted with the system */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data storage system interacted with */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date the interaction occurred */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., view, modify) */,
  "interaction_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the interaction */,
  "interaction_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the interaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_interactions_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES data_storage_systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_interactions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data storage system this setting belongs to */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "setting_description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "setting_type" TEXT /* Type of the setting value (e.g., integer, string, boolean) */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_settings_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES data_storage_systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "indices" (
  "index_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each index */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data storage system this index belongs to */,
  "index_name" TEXT /* Name of the index */,
  "index_type" TEXT /* Type of index (e.g., primary, secondary) */,
  "index_description" TEXT /* Description of the index */,
  "index_status" TEXT /* Status of the index (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("index_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_indices_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES data_storage_systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ttl" (
  "ttl_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each TTL */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data storage system this TTL belongs to */,
  "ttl_value" INTEGER /* Value of the TTL */,
  "ttl_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the TTL (e.g., seconds, minutes) */,
  "ttl_description" TEXT /* Description of the TTL setting */,
  "ttl_status" TEXT /* Status of the TTL setting (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ttl_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ttl_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES data_storage_systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role assignment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date of the log entry */,
  "log_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the log */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of the log (e.g., info, error, warning) */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message of the log */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
